sources: NIST SP 800-171 Revision 2, Appendix D (Mapping Tables D-1 through D-14)
references/fedramp-gap.md (Appendix D reproduction, verified against primary PDF)
NIST SP 800-53 Revision 5 FedRAMP Moderate baseline (OSCAL, usnistgov/oscal-content)
verified: 2026-07-03: 110 requirements; Appendix D control mappings; FedRAMP Moderate baseline membership from NIST OSCAL profile
iso_27001_notes: NIST SP 800-171 Rev 2 Appendix D maps each requirement to ISO/IEC 27001 controls where a direct mapping exists; some cells read 'No direct mapping'. For ISO/IEC 27001:2022 alignment at the 800-53 layer, use the NIST OLIR crosswalk from SP 800-53 Rev 5 (see SOURCES.md). Mappings are indicative, not one-to-one equivalencies.

requirements.name: Transaction & Function Control
requirements.notes: Transaction and function control (shares AC-2/3/17 with 3.1.1)

requirements.name: System Security Plan
requirements.notes: System security plan (mapped from PL family)

requirements.name: Application Execution Policy
rev5_notes: Rev 4 restricted allowlisting to High baseline (Appendix D footnote 32); confirm CSP Rev 5 authorization reflects current baseline treatment.

requirements.name: Replay-Resistant Authentication
rev5_notes: Rev 5 Moderate includes replay resistance for non-privileged accounts; Rev 4 Moderate did not (Appendix D footnote 33).
Rev 5 Moderate includes replay resistance for non-privileged accounts; Rev 4 Moderate did not (Appendix D footnote 33).

requirements.name: Media Inspection
requirements.notes: Check diagnostic media for malicious code

requirements.name: Protect Backups
requirements.notes: Backup CUI confidentiality. CP-9 is the Contingency Planning family control folded into MP in Appendix D.

requirements.name: Security Engineering
requirements.notes: Security engineering principles. SA-8 is the System and Services Acquisition family control folded into SC in Appendix D.

requirements.name: Network Communication by Exception
requirements.notes: Deny by default, allow by exception
